---
title: "Paper Presentations"
sidebar: assignments
---


## Overview

Each student has been assigned one journal publication to present in class. The
expected structure of the paper presentations is outlined in this [Slideshow Template](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1hOlRmBfrAnXku6-Oq61sh0wkvnBqDTAw3tIEAXaStxw/edit). 
Students can use any slide show presentation software they wish, but should follow the 
presentation structure of the template.
A detailed presentation schedule is available in the internal [Course Planning Sheet](https://elearn.ucr.edu/courses/224081) sheet (see Columns Q-R of Projects Tab). 
The grading of both the paper and project presentations includes
anonymous feedback from all students as well as the instructor, where
understanding of the material, clarity of the oral presentations and critical
thinking are the main grading criteria. The grading forms will be provided in the 
Presentation Schedule (internal google sheet) shortly before the presentations start
on May 14th and May 19th. The following lists the assigned papers organized by 
course project topics.


## Publications organized by course projects

All references in [Paperpile](https://paperpile.com/shared/GEN242-2026-fm6L56jC5QIiEwSUq3827kg)


### RNA-Seq Aligners

1. Kim D, Langmead B, Salzberg SL (2015) HISAT: a fast spliced aligner with low memory requirements. Nat Methods 12: 357–360. [PubMed](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/25751142/)

2. Bray NL, Pimentel H, Melsted P, Pachter L (2016) Near-optimal probabilistic RNA-seq quantification. Nat Biotechnol 34: 525–527. [Pubmed](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/27043002/)

### DEG Analysis Methods

3. Love MI, Huber W, Anders S (2014) Moderated estimation of fold change and dispersion for RNA-seq data with DESeq2. Genome Biol 15: 550. [PubMed](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/25516281)

4. Guo Y, Li C-I, Ye F, Shyr Y (2013) Evaluation of read count based RNAseq analysis methods. BMC Genomics 14 Suppl 8: S2. [PubMed](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/24564449/) 

5. Zhou X, Lindsay H, Robinson MD (2014) Robustly detecting differential expression in RNA sequencing data using observation weights. Nucleic Acids Res 42: e91. [PubMed](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/24753412/)

### Functional Interpretation

6. Subramanian A, Tamayo P, Mootha VK, Mukherjee S, Ebert BL, Gillette MA, Paulovich A, Pomeroy SL, Golub TR, Lander ES, et al (2005) Gene set enrichment analysis: a knowledge-based approach for interpreting genome-wide expression profiles. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A 102: 15545–15550. [PubMed](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/16199517/)

7. Schubert M, Klinger B, Klünemann M, Sieber A, Uhlitz F, Sauer S, Garnett MJ, Blüthgen N, Saez-Rodriguez J (2018) Perturbation-response genes reveal signaling footprints in cancer gene expression. Nat Commun 9: 20. [PubMed](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/29295995/)   

8. Hänzelmann S, Castelo R, Guinney J (2013) GSVA: gene set variation analysis for microarray and RNA-seq data. BMC Bioinformatics 14: 7. [PubMed](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/23323831/)

### AI/ML Classification

9. Díaz-Uriarte R, Alvarez de Andrés S (2006) Gene selection and classification of microarray data using random forest. BMC Bioinformatics 7: 3. [PubMed](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/16398926/)

10. Strobl C, Boulesteix A-L, Zeileis A, Hothorn T (2007) Bias in random forest variable importance measures: illustrations, sources and a solution. BMC Bioinformatics 8: 25. [PubMed](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/17254353/)

11. Lundberg SM, Erion G, Chen H, DeGrave A, Prutkin JM, Nair B, Katz R, Himmelfarb J, Bansal N, Lee S-I (2020) From local explanations to global understanding with explainable AI for trees. Nat Mach Intell 2: 56–67. [PubMed](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32607472/)

12. Chen T, Guestrin C (2016) XGBoost: A Scalable Tree Boosting System. arXiv [csLG]. doi: 10.48550/arXiv.1603.0275. [arXiv](https://arxiv.org/abs/1603.02754)

### Single-cell Genomics: Clustering Significance & Dimensionality Reduction

13. Grabski IN, Street K, Irizarry RA (2023) Significance analysis for clustering with single-cell RNA-sequencing data. Nat Methods 20: 1196–1202. [PubMed](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37429993/)

14. Huang H, Wang Y, Rudin C, Browne EP (2022) Towards a comprehensive evaluation of dimension reduction methods for transcriptomic data visualization. Commun Biol 5: 719. [PubMed](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35853932/)

### Single-cell Genomics: Cell-type Annotation & Trajectory Inference

### Multi-omics: Integration & Pathways

### Multi-omics: Classification & Network analysis
